commands.review_summary:
The PR changes memory-core status identity resolution and tests so local embedding indexes configured with only `memorySearch.local.modelPath` compare against the local model path instead of `settings.model`.

PR surface: Source +19, Tests +41. Total +60 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-level: current main persists the initialized local provider model path but status-only identity expects `settings.model` or fallback before provider initialization. I did not run the real Docker/GGUF CLI path in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] This needs author or maintainer follow-up for the plugin-contract scope and real behavior proof; the missing external setup proof makes it unsuitable for an automated repair lane.

target.title: fix(memory): keep local embedding indexes clean when only local.modelPath is set
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #91001. A local (llama.cpp) embeddings index configured solely via `local.modelPath` was permanently reported as `Dirty: yes`, pausing vector search even right after a successful `openclaw memory index --force`.

**Root cause:** the llama.cpp adapter derives the provider's model identity from `local.modelPath` and ignores top-level `settings.model` (`extensions/llama-cpp/src/embedding-provider.ts` → `buildMemoryCreateOptions`). So a built index stores `meta.model` as the gguf path (e.g. `/…/embeddinggemma-300M-Q8_0.gguf`). But the plain-status identity mirror in `extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-sync-ops.ts` only consulted `settings.model` (empty) and fell back to the adapter default, so the expected model never matched the stored model → perpetual `mismatched` → `Dirty: yes`, vector search paused.

**Fix:** when no live provider is initialized (status path), mirror the adapter by preferring `local.modelPath` for local-transport providers when computing the expected model identity. Remote providers are unchanged. No-`modelPath` local configs still fall back to the adapter default, which is exactly what the adapter uses, so they keep matching.

Net diff: source +15/-14 (one inlined block replaced by a small, documented, lazily-invoked helper); tests +47.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** local embeddings index shows `Dirty: yes` / "index was built for model <path>, expected …" forever when configured with only `local.modelPath`.
- **Real environment tested:** OpenClaw source checkout, Node v22.22.3, Vitest, `extension-memory` project.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/memory/index.test.ts`
- **Evidence after fix:** added regression test "keeps local llama.cpp indexes clean when only local.modelPath is configured". Before the source fix it failed with `index was built for model /models/embeddinggemma-300M-Q8_0.gguf, expected ` (empty expected) → `mismatched`. After the fix the index identity resolves to `{ status: "valid" }` and `dirty === false`.
- **Observed result after fix:** `Test Files 1 passed`, `Tests 49 passed (49)`. `oxfmt --check` clean; editor lint clean.
- **What was not tested:** no end-to-end build against a real gguf model file (no node-llama-cpp model available locally), and no local structured autoreview (no review-engine CLI installed on this machine). One unrelated test, `manager-sync-ops.startup-catchup.test.ts > "retries transient session transcript reads"`, fails — verified **pre-existing on clean `origin/main`** (shelved this change → still fails) and in a session-catch-up path untouched by this PR.

## Verification

```
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/memory/index.test.ts
# Test Files 1 passed (1) / Tests 49 passed (49)
```
target.author: tiffanychum
